chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P.No.D-6994 of 2022 ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE(S) OF JUDGE(S) ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 1.For order on CMA No.892/2026 2.For hearing of main case. 21-01-2026 Mr,.Usman Farooq, Advocate for the Petitioner. Ms.Mehreen Ibrahim, D.A.G. Mr.Abdul Samad, Advocate for NADRA. --- Petitioner Mujeeb-ur-Rehman has filed the present constitutional petition under Article 199 of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, seeking directions to the respondents to unblock his CNIC without any further delay. As per the statement made by the learned counsel for NADRA, and report of V&R Branch, Regional Head Office (RHO) NADRA, Karachi, that the CNIC bearing No. 42201-7706909-1 of the petitioner has been digitally impounded on account of his non-appearance before the Verification Board. However, learned counsel for NADRA has stated that the respondents are ready and willing to unblock the CNIC, subject to the petitioner’s appearance before the concerned Zone “C”, Malir Karachi. Learned counsel for the petitioner agrees to the proposed course and undertakes that the petitioner shall appear before the relevant NADRA branch. It is further agreed that, upon such appearance and after verification, if the CNIC of the petitioner is found to be genuine and requisite supporting documents are provided, the same shall be unblocked by NADRA in accordance with law. In view of the above, the petition stands disposed of in the aforesaid terms.

It is further agreed that, upon such appearance and after verification, if the CNIC of the petitioner is found to be genuine and requisite supporting documents are provided, the same shall be unblocked by NADRA in accordance with law. In view of the above, the petition stands disposed of in the aforesaid terms. Judge Judge nasir
